LHX.sentence: L3Harris is flat today — it's trading at a premium to other defense contractors, but the military spending tailwinds haven't changed.

ASML.sentence: ASML dropped about 3% despite being the world's only supplier of advanced chipmaking machines, which suggests investors are just taking some profits after a good run.

LIN.sentence: Linde is up slightly and steady as usual — the industrial gas business keeps humming along with its long-term contracts, though profit margins aren't quite as strong as they used to be.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down a bit ahead of earnings, where everyone will be watching how fast the Azure cloud business is growing from all the AI demand.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is looking cheaper after being down lately — the lab equipment giant is trading at its lowest price relative to earnings in years, which could be an opportunity.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is holding steady, though the dividend isn't as attractive as it usually gets since interest rates are still high.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow dropped about 3% but the business fundamentals look solid — big companies keep signing up for their AI workflow tools and those contracts tend to be sticky.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is up slightly, with investors starting to recognize that its commodity trading business is more valuable than just owning mines.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is down about 4% and sitting very close to its lowest point in a year, which historically has been a decent entry point.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is quiet and down slightly — not much happening with the Mexican peso or Latin American soda sales right now.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is paying a massive 61% dividend yield, which either means oil profits are unsustainable or the stock is deeply undervalued — probably worth investigating.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk jumped nearly 10% today and is trading below the cost of replacing its container ships, which suggests the market thinks global shipping is in worse shape than it actually is.
